data.q: What is the primary purpose of the Standard PHP Library (SPL)?
o: To provide a set of functions for interacting with MySQL databases
To provide a collection of interfaces and classes designed to solve common tool and data structure problems
To replace the core Zend Engine compilation layer
To manage external composer dependencies automatically
data.e: SPL is a built-in extension that provides standard design patterns, advanced data structures (like heaps, stacks, and queues), and object-oriented file iterators.

data.q: Which SPL interface allows an object to be looped over using a standard `foreach` construct?
data.e: `Traversable` is the internal abstract base interface that dictates an object can be iterated. In user-land code, you implement its sub-interfaces: `Iterator` or `IteratorAggregate`.

data.q: What unique capability does implementing the `ArrayAccess` interface grant to a custom PHP object?
o: It converts the object into a JSON string automatically
It allows the object to be accessed using standard array bracket syntax (e.g., $obj['key'])
It forces all object properties to be integers
It allows the object to be passed directly to array_merge()
data.e: `ArrayAccess` requires implementing four methods: `offsetExists`, `offsetGet`, `offsetSet`, and `offsetUnset`, allowing objects to mimic regular array syntax.

data.q: Which SPL data structure class represents a discrete, memory-efficient Doubly Linked List that optimizes sequential push/pop operations?
data.e: `SplDoublyLinkedList` serves as the underlying engine for both `SplStack` and `SplQueue`, providing low-level memory architecture for node manipulation.

data.q: Why is an `SplFixedArray` often preferred over a standard native PHP array for large, numerical datasets?
o: It supports string keys more efficiently
It is strictly bound to a fixed size and stores elements sequentially in memory, using significantly less RAM and offering faster lookup speeds
It can be shared across separate server processes
data.e: Standard PHP arrays are actually associative hash tables under the hood. `SplFixedArray` utilizes a classic C-style contiguous memory block, drastically lowering structural memory overhead.

data.q: What is the specialized function of the `SplPriorityQueue` structure?
o: It behaves like a standard FIFO queue but elements are dequeued based on an explicit priority score assigned to them
It limits the queue to only contain high-priority system files
It processes elements inside a parallel asynchronous thread loop
data.e: `SplPriorityQueue` uses a max-heap implementation to maintain internal sorted order, ensuring that items with the highest priority score are extracted first.

data.q: Which class would you use to recursively iterate through nested folders and list deep sub-directory files in an object-oriented loop?
data.e: `RecursiveDirectoryIterator` maps the nested folder paths, while `RecursiveIteratorIterator` flattens that recursive depth into a single linear loop context.

data.q: What major architectural optimization does a 'Generator' (using the `yield` keyword) offer over returning an SPL `ArrayIterator`?
o: Generators compile directly into machine instructions
Generators utilize lazy-evaluation to stream data values one at a time, preventing massive memory consumption when processing huge files
Generators allow bi-directional loops over the data set
data.e: Instead of loading an entire dataset into memory to populate an iterator object, a Generator pauses execution state and yields values on demand, handling millions of rows using virtually zero extra RAM.

data.q: Which SPL exception class is designed to handle invalid arguments passed to a function that don't match expected criteria?
data.e: `InvalidArgumentException` belongs to the LogicException branch of SPL, indicating a developer error where arguments do not match the expected structural characteristics.

data.q: What structural pattern does the `SplObserver` and `SplSubject` interface pairing implement natively in PHP?
Which SPL iterator allows you to wrap another iterator and filter out elements based on custom logic implemented in an `accept()` method?
data.e: `FilterIterator` is an abstract class where you define a custom `accept()` method returning true or false. Elements that return false are automatically skipped during loop iteration.

data.q: What happens when you instantiate a standard `SplFileObject` on a CSV file and set the flag `SplFileObject::READ_CSV`?
o: It converts the file to an Excel format
It allows you to iterate over the file lines directly as pre-parsed native PHP arrays of column values
It loads the entire file into a database table
It locks the file from being edited by other system programs
data.e: `SplFileObject` treats files as objects. Enabling the `READ_CSV` flag instructs the internal pointer to automatically trigger `fgetcsv()` on every iteration step, cleanly parsing rows.
